element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Differentiates character playstyles; German suplexes, power bombs, ninja splits
Tests accumulated combat skills against stronger opponent variants
Forces spatial awareness beyond combat mechanics
Risk-reward mechanic: requires taking damage to activate burst

element_introduction_order.element: Maze-like stage layouts with multiple paths
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Stage 3 (The Drain) and Stage 9 (Airshed) in Western version
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Introduces navigation complexity and dead-end exploration

element_introduction_order.element: Final boss Dieter/Iceman with extendable staff
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Ultimate difficulty spike combining all learned mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Stage completion and transition to next area
Partner AI assistance against multiple enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemy waves in linear combat arenas
Environmental hazards (spikes, rotating platforms) restricting movement
Low health state triggering Angry Mode availability
Maze-like stage navigation (Western version) creating disorientation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based with escalating difficulty per stage. Early stages establish mechanics with manageable enemy density. Mid-game introduces environmental complications. Late-game combines dense enemy encounters with spatial navigation puzzles. Final boss serves as comprehensive skill test.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Dead-end paths in maze stages (Western version Stage 3 and 9) suggest optional exploration areas with potential pickups

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory stage teaches basic punch/kick/throw mechanics with moderate enemy pressure. Partner character provides backup. Single-opponent encounters establish combat rhythm.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy density increases significantly. Environmental hazards introduced (Kazan's rotating dojo with floor spikes). Maze-like navigation elements (Western version) complicate progression. Multiple enemy types require tactical positioning.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Sustained high-pressure encounters combining dense enemy waves with environmental obstacles. Final boss Dieter combines extended reach (staff), mobility, and aggressive AI requiring mastery of dodge, throw, and weapon mechanics.
notable_spikes: Stage 3 transition to maze layout (Western version) disrupts expected linear progression
Kazan boss stage with rotating walls and spike floor hazards
Final boss Dieter with unprecedented reach and unpredictable staff extension patterns

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 5 playable character has unique boss stage environment: Hack fights in steel cage platform, Slash on construction platform that rises/lowers, Lord J in temple court, Kazan in rotating dojo with spikes, Wendy Milan in wrestling ring with ropework mechanics. This prevents stage repetition despite linear level progression.

Western version modified Japanese original by converting linear stages 3 and 9 into maze-like layouts with multiple paths and dead ends. This increases exploration complexity and navigation-based tension in late game without changing combat fundamentals.

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Partner random selection with boss escalation
notable_level_patterns.description: CPU assigns random partner from roster for stage progression. After stage completion, remaining selectable characters (or their clones per Japanese lore) become bosses for first three levels. Final stage presents Dieter as ultimate opponent, creating narrative arc where allies become adversaries.

Static arena-based combat dominates early stages. Mid-game introduces interactive elements (rotating platforms, spike hazards, platform elevation changes). Late-game maze stages add spatial navigation as hazard type.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early: simple urban environments. Mid: Kazan's dojo. Late: Drain and Airshed mazes
